SET FOR JULY 19, 2012 AT 09:30AM IN DEPT. 302.
DECLARATION OF DELORIS COLLIER IN SUPP OF OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO COMPEL ARB FILED BY PLAINTIFF COLLIER, DELORIS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS VPSF, INC. DBA REAL FOOD COMPANY AND TOM SERAFICA'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY COURT PROCEEDINGS AND TO DENY ENTRY OF DEFAULT REQUEST IS GRANTED. THIS ACTION IS NOT EXCLUDED FROM THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. ASSUMING THAT THE DISCOVERY CLAUSE IS AN UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISION, THAT CLAUSE IS SEVERED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT ALLOWS FOR THE RECOVERY OF FEHA ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE FRCP CLAUSE IS NOT AN UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

Ntc Of Petition And Petition Of Deft To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings And To Deny Entry Of Default Request
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph P. Breen, Esq. of Strazulo Fitzgerald LLP, Tel. 949.225.4473, appearing for defendants and moving party;
John Scarpino, Esq. of the Law Office of John Scarpino, Tel. 415.252.8552, appearing for the plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs VPSF, INC. DBA REAL FOOD COMPANY and TOM SERAFICA's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings And To Deny Entry Of Default Request is Granted. This action is not excluded from the scope of the arbitration clause. Assuming that the discovery clause is an unconscionable provision, that clause is severed. The arbitration agreement allows for the recovery of FEHA attorney's fees. The FRCP clause is not an unconscionable provision. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Continued To Jul-25-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610
